mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly lower when both INDPRO and HOUST decline concurrently compared to periods where only one indicator falls, or the joint signal's Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of cooling consumer credit on corporate margins, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when FEDFUNDS spikes alongside declining RSAFS, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes insignificant in a rolling regression over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as equity markets price in stronger macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: The Rank IC between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP quarters do not yield significantly higher forward returns than negative quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 per month, it signals robust labor market expansion that fuels wage-driven consumption and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher in months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 200,000 compared to baseline, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with stable consumer prices indicates healthy supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, which reduces discount rate uncertainty and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns do not significantly outperform when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL remains flat, or the joint signal's predictive power drops below a 0.04 Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where housing starts peak and unemployment stabilizes near 6.5 percent, credit-sensitive demand indicators like revolving consumer credit begin to decouple from GDP growth, signaling an imminent earnings downgrade cycle that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST peaks and UNRATE remains below 7.0 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST peaks alongside stable unemployment and declining credit, or the regime-specific signal fails to generate a negative Rank IC exceeding -0.05 over a 2-year window.
